titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Diahann Carroll Papers PASC-M.0260
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Kevin Awakuni and Alexandra Apolloni, October 2014; machine-readable finding aid created by Caroline Cubé.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2021 January 11.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-01-11 17:02:32 -0800.

physdesc.extent: (24 boxes, 2 shoe boxes, 92 flat boxes, and 8 oversize flat boxes)

did.abstract: Over the course of her career, actress and singer Diahann Carroll has been involved in a number of stage, motion picture, and television projects as well a business and philanthropic activities. The bulk of the collection includes musical arrangements, clippings, and photographs documenting assorted stage, motion picture, and television projects. Additionally there is a small amount correspondence, scrapbooks, awards, and ephemera.
did.physloc: Stored off-site. All requests to access special collections material must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS DIGITAL AND AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: This collection contains both processed and unprocessed digital and audiovisual materials. For information about the access status of the material that you are looking for, refer to the Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements note at the series and file levels. All requests to access processed digital and audiovisual materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical objects belong to UCLA Library Special Collections. All other rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Diahann Carroll papers (Collection PASC-M 260). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.
Processed by Kevin Awakuni and Alexandra Apolloni under the supervision of Julie Graham, October 2014.

odd.head: UCLA Catalog Record ID
odd.p: UCLA Catalog Record ID: 5971344

bioghist.head: Biography/History
bioghist.p: Carol Diahann Johnson (later changed to Diahann Carroll), was born in the Bronx, New York on July 17, 1935. At a young age she received a scholarship to attend New York's High School of Music and Art. While studying sociology at NYU, she modeled and began singing in New York nightclubs and on television. In 1954, she debuted on Broadway with Pearl Bailey in House of Flowers, and received a Tony nomination for her performance. The same year, she appeared in the motion picture Carmen Jones. In 1962 she received a Tony Award for her performance in No Strings, the first for an African American woman.

In 1968, Carroll became the first black actress in television history to star in her own series, Julia, for NBC. She won a Golden Globe (1968) and earned an Emmy nomination (1969), for her performance on the series. Public criticism that the show was not a realistic portrayal of African American life led Carroll to decline to renew her contract with the show in 1970. In early 1974 Carroll was featured in the motion picture Claudine for which she earned an Academy Award nomination. In 1984, Carroll joined the television show Dynasty as Dominique Devereaux, the first major prime-time soap opera role for an African American female.

Over the course of her career Carroll has been involved in a number of live performances, stage, motion picture, and television projects. Stage credits include Agnes of God, Love Letters, Same Time Next Year, and Sunset Boulevard. Carroll's film credits include Porgy and Bess (1959), Goodbye Again (1961), Paris Blues (1961), Hurry Sundown (1967), The Split (1968), The Five Heartbeats (1991), and Eve's Bayou (1997). Her numerous television credits include series and specials such as The Colbys, General Electric Theater, Lonesome Dove, Naked City, Peter Gunn, and Touched by an Angel, and TV movies such as Having Our Say, The Natalie Cole Story, A Perry Mason Mystery, and Sister, Sister.

In 1997, Carroll created a signature line of clothes and a line of wigs for African American women. The following year, Carroll was diagnosed with breast cancer and became an outspoken advocate for early detection, and she has served as spokeswoman for the National Women's Cancer Research Alliance. Diahann Carroll has earned many honors including the Women in Film Crystal and Lucy awards, NAACP Image award for best actress, and induction into Academy of Television Arts and Sciences Hall of Fame.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of materials related to the career of actress and singer, Diahann Carroll. The bulk of the collection includes musical arrangements, clippings, and photographs documenting assorted stage, motion picture, and television projects. Additionally there is a small amount correspondence, scrapbooks, awards, and ephemera. Musical arrangements represent assorted stage performances including solo performances and performances with Vic Damone. Among the motion picture productions represented are Carmen Jones and Claudine; television projects documented include Dynasty , Having Our Say , Julia , and Lonesome Dove ; stage performances represented are House of Flowers, No Strings, Sunset Boulevard, and Diahann Carroll and Vic Damon in Concert. With few exceptions, notably missing from the collection are scripts and production information representing Carroll's numerous acting roles for stage, film, and television.

The bulk of the correspondence are notes sent to Diahann Carroll from correspondents such as Judy Collins, Bing Crosby, Sammy Davis, Jr., Berry Gordy, Quincy Jones, Henry Kissinger, and Barbara Walters, among others. Additionally there are invitations to attend White House receptions from George H. Bush, Jimmy Carter, and Bill Clinton and a thank you note from John F. Kennedy. Also included are materials from Diahann Carroll's fashion venture projects, materials documenting what appears to be an unpublished autobiography book project, a copyedited manuscript for the book Diahann: An Autobiography, and a very small amount of material related to Carroll's personal life.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p:
Arranged in the following series:
Personal and Biographical
Motion Picture Projects
Television Projects
Stage Projects
Musical Arrangements
Fashion Projects
Correspondence
Photographs
Scrapbooks
Book Projects
Awards
Printed Material
Sound Recordings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The series consists of a small number of items related to personal events and/or relationships in Carroll's private life. Included are materials representing her education, her Mother Mabel Johnson, her daughter Suzanne Kay, and her marriages to Fred Glusman, Robert DeLeon, and Vic Damone. There is no specific arrangement to the series.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Two unaddressed cards. Front is image of Diahann Carroll and Vic Damone and family from their wedding.

did.unittitle: Damone, Andrea - Thanksgiving card to Diahann Carroll
Damone, Vic - personal notes to Diahann Carroll

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Invitations and two snapshots (one including Don Rickles).

did.unittitle: Damone, Vic and Diahann Carroll - clippings
DeLeon, Robert and Diahann Carroll marriage

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Clippings, photographs and slides. Some photographs and slides may be from Carroll-DeLeon nuptials.

did.unittitle: DeLeon, Robert remembrance - telegram and memorial folder

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Loose pages covering from January to December 17.

did.unittitle: Diahann Carroll Junior High School records

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Certificate of completion and report card.

did.unittitle: Diahann Carroll New York University - withdrawl records

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Letters and transcript of classes regarding withdrawl from New York University.

did.unittitle: Diahann Carroll partial maternal family history
Glusman, Freddy and Diahann Carroll wedding

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Invitation, announcement and marriage certificate.

did.unittitle: Johnson, Mabel - birthday card to Diahann Carroll
Kay, Monte and Diahann Carroll wedding pictures (4 items)
Kay, Suzanne - 21st birthday party invitation and photographs with parents

odd.head: General note
odd.p: With Daughters of Celebrities are Making their Own Mark. Ebony Oct 1991.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Invitation, clipping, and class portrait.

did.unittitle: Kay, Suzanne and Diahann Carroll - proof sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The series consists of a small amount of stills and clippings documenting Diahann Carrolls film career. The productions represented are Carmen Jones, Claudine, Hurry Sundown, Paris Blues, The Split, and Uptown Express. Notably absent from the series are scripts for each of these projects except Uptown Express. The series is arranged alphabetically by project titles.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Still signed Sidney Poitier.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The series contains materials related to assorted television projects in which Diahann Carroll was involved. The bulk of the series are clippings, publicity photographs, and memorabilia. Among the productions represented are The Diahann Carroll Show , Dynasty , Having Our Say , Julia , Lonesome Dove , and Carroll's appearance on the Mike Douglas Show . Notably absent from the series are scripts for most of the projects. The series is arranged alphabetically by project titles.

did.unittitle: TV Guide cover featuring Diahann Carroll (mounted)
[ABC Stage 67: A Time for Laughter] - photographs

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Behind the scene shots; appears to be, ABC Stage 67: Time for Laughter.

did.unittitle: C'est La Vie - Ebony magazine cover and photograph featuring Carroll and Maurice Chevalier
Diahann Carroll Show (The) - publicity stills (12 items)
Dynasty - TV Guide cover featuring Diahann Carroll (mounted)
Francis Albert Sinatra Does His Thing - press clipping
Intimate Portraits - executed contract (release) and notes
Julia - Dallas Morning News TV Magazine with cover story
Julia - Look magazine piece on Diahann Carrol

odd.head: General note
odd.p: In original box. Stand up dolls and custom pieces are unperferated.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Stills and Emmy Award-related photographs. With two photographs of Carroll, Sidney Portier, and Ted Lange that appear to be from the United Negro College Fund Lou Rawls Parade of Stars event.

did.unittitle: Julia - TV Guide cover featuring Diahann Carroll (mounted)
Julia - TV Guide cover featuring Diahann Carroll and Marc Copage (mounted)
Julia - TV Guide cover portrait featuring Diahann Carroll

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Appears related to Julia.

did.unittitle: Julia - Variety advertisement recognizing National Nielsen Award win, published by Creative Management Associates
Julia: Mama's Man - pilot script (one original, one photocopy)

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Fragile. From Carroll's appearance on the show.

did.unittitle: Rockette: Holiday Tribute to Radio City Music Hall - publicity still

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The Stage Project Series contains a small number of items documenting stage productions in which Diahann Carroll was involved. Included are photographs, clippings, ephemera and occasional correspondence. Among the projects represented are House of Flowers, No Strings, Sunset Boulevard, and Diahann Carroll and Vic Damon in Concert. Notably absent from the series are scripts for most of the projects. The series is arranged alphabetically by project titles. The Scrapbook series may also contain material related to stage projects.

did.unittitle: Agnes of God - playbills and souvenir program
Bob Hope Concert - photographs and clipping

odd.p: Two programs and a photograph of a publicity billboard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The series contains musical arrangements for Diahann Carroll singing performances. The series is arranged by Diahann Carroll solo performances and performances with Vic Damone. Files within each series are arranged alphabetically by song title when known.

did.unittitle: Diahann Carroll and Vic Damone arrangements
Costume change/When your lover has gone
Diahann Carroll intro (Sat. night at the palace)
Do you hear what I hear
He was too good to me
How do you keep the music playing
How do you keep the music playing?
I didn't know what time it was
I get along without you very well
I Think I'm Going to Make It
I think I'm gonna make it
I'd rather leave while I'm in love
I'd Rather Leave While I'm in Love
I'm gonna make it all the way
It's a grand night for singing
I've never been a fool like this before
Music that makes me dance for Frank Sinatra special

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The series documents Diahann Carroll's fashion venture projects. Included are photographs, clippings, transparencies, publicity materials and textual documents. The series is arranged alphabetically by file titles supplied by processor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The bulk of the series comprises notes sent to Diahann Carroll. Among the correspondents are Judy Collins, Bing Crosby, Sammy Davis, Jr., Berry Gordy, Quincy Jones, Henry Kissinger, and Barbara Walters, among others. Additionally there are invitations to attend White House receptions from George H. Bush, Jimmy Carter, and Bill Clinton and a thank you note from John F. Kennedy. The series is arranged alphabetically by correspondent.

Bush, George H. - letter and reception invitation

Kissinger, Henry A. - thank you notes
Note from Berry Gordy to Diahann Carroll
Note from Suzanne Kay to Diahann Carroll

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Appears related to No Strings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The series consists of photographs, photograph albums, slides and negatives related to Carroll's professional and personal life. These materials do not appear related to other identified motion picture, television or live performance projects in the collection. Included are professional photographs from Carroll's early career and personal photograph albums of snapshots of and with family and friends. There is no specific arrangement to the series.

odd.p: With snapshot with Liza Minelli.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The series consists of scrapbooks related to Carroll's professional life and may contain clippings and/or photographs. The bulk of projects represented are stage appearances including Agnes of God , No Strings , Boca Pops , and appearances with Vic Damone, among others. The scrapbooks are arranged in a loose chronological order.

did.unittitle: Diahann Carroll scrapbook pages of clippings and loose photographs from early career
Diahann Carroll scrapbook of career related clippings

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Fragile and heavy.

did.unittitle: Diahann Carroll scrapbook of career related clippings
Diahann Carroll scrapbook of career related clippings
Diahann Carroll scrapbook of career related clippings, notes, photographs, and ephemera

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Mostly stage work. Anges of God, No Strings and more.

did.unittitle: Diahann Carroll scrapbook of career related clippings and photographs

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Assorted projects including Vic Damone, Boca Pops, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The series contains materials related to at least two Diahann Carroll book projects. Included are micro cassette recordings, transcriptions of interviews with Carroll, and holograph and typewritten notes all compiled by Laura Morton for what appears to be an unpublished autobiography project. Also included is a copyedited manuscript for Diahann: An Autobiography by Diahann Carroll and Ross Firestone. There are also 60-plus audio tapes that appear related to an unnamed biography project. There is no specific arrangement to the series.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS DIGITAL AND AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: This collection contains both processed and unprocessed digital and audiovisual materials. For information about the access status of the material that you are looking for, refer to the Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements note at the series and file levels. All requests to access processed digital and audiovisual materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials are available for access. All requests to access digital materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Photocopy of copyedited pages. Appears to be autobiographical. May be related to a book project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The Awards Series contains photographs and certificates documenting honors and awards presented to Diahann Carroll. Among the tributes documented are the Tony Award for No Strings , the Women in Film Lucy Awards, the Ebony Achievement Award, and the NAACP Image Award, among others. The series is arranged alphabetically by award title.

did.unittitle: City of Los Angeles Certificate of Commendation - signed by Tom Bradley
Congratulations announcement from Dynasty for NAACP Image
Costume Designers' Guild Best Dressed American Actress award
Harper's Bazaar Medallion of Recognition - certificate
Pacific Pioneers Broadcasters Award - program and faxed tributes

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Tributes from Hal Holbrook, Dennis James, Richard Kiley, Burgess Meredith, Burt Reynolds. With personal notes to Carroll from Aaron Spelling and Dionne Warwick.

did.unittitle: Performing Arts Center of Los Angeles Honors - certificate

odd.head: General note
odd.p: For A Different World.

did.unittitle: Tony Awards photographs for No Strings
Women in Film Lucy Awards - photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The series contains news clippings and magazine articles related to and/or about Diahann Carroll. Included are general publicity write-ups, biographical in nature, which do not appear to be related to any specific television, film, stage, or business venture projects. There is no specific arrangement to the series.

did.unittitle: Academy of Television Arts & Sciences annual report

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Photograph of Carroll in the publication.

did.unittitle: Breast cancer - clippings featuring Diahann Carroll

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Media accounts and awareness campaign.

did.unittitle: Breast cancer Mother's Day Mammograms event - media report and clippings
Clipping sent from Mabel Johnson regarding birth of Suzanne Kay
Diahann Carroll - pencil sketch by Michael G. Reagan (1 item)
Diahann Carroll Celebrity Invitational Golf Tournament memorabilia

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Souvenier guide and oversized sticker.

did.unittitle: Diahann Carroll portfolio and promotional material
Diahann Carroll: Tribute to Ethel Waters clippings
Ebony magazine piece about Carroll and Robert Deleon

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Titled: We're Still Very Much In Love.

did.unittitle: Hola! magazine piece about Carroll and Vic Damone (in Spanish)
House Beautiful with feature article about Carroll's home
Invitation - Sunday Evening with Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee
Jet magazine - first magazine cover appearance
New York Apartment - clippings related to litigation
Sunday Evening with Diahann Carroll Lincoln Center advertisement
There Will Be Some Changes Made - published sheet music

odd.head: General note
odd.p: As recorded by top recording artitsts including Diahann Carroll.

did.unittitle: Women in Film Crystal Awards - program

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: CONTAINS DIGITAL AND AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: This collection contains both processed and unprocessed digital and audiovisual materials. For information about the access status of the material that you are looking for, refer to the Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements note at the series and file levels. All requests to access processed digital and audiovisual materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: The series consists of 45 rpm, 33 1/3 rpm LP and 78 rpm disc sound recordings. Includes recordings of musical works or stage productions which feature Carroll and/or other performers. There is no specific arrangement to the series.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: COLLECTION CONTAINS AUDIOVISUAL MATERIALS: Audiovisual materials in this collection will require assessment and possible digitization for safe access. To review these audiovisual materials, you must notify the UCLA Library Special Collections Reference Desk in advance of your visit.

odd.p: 12-inch reel-to-reel tape.
